ogTitle: Main Ridge vs Maintongoon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Main Ridge and Maintongoon.
heading: Main Ridge vs Maintongoon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Main Ridge, Victoria and Maintongoon, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Main Ridge has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Maintongoon (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Main Ridge or Maintongoon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Main Ridge has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Maintongoon scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Main Ridge scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Maintongoon scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Main Ridge or Maintongoon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Main Ridge has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
